perturbation.original_text: Client will pay TrueLink's fees for requested Hosting Services and Support Services pursuant to the schedule of charges set forth on Exhibits "A" and "B" attached hereto, (but not less than $____ per month for the Hosting Services and $____ per month for Support Services). For any other services agreed to between the parties, Client will pay TrueLink fees which will be determined by the parties and will vary depending on the services utilized. TrueLink will provide Client with an invoice for the requested Hosting Services and Support Services and any other agreed-upon services on a monthly basis. Invoices will be paid within 15 days of receipt.
perturbation.changed_text: Client will pay TrueLink's fees for requested Hosting Services and Support Services pursuant to the schedule of charges set forth on Exhibits "A" and "B" attached hereto. For any other services agreed to between the parties, Client will pay TrueLink fees which will be determined by the parties and will vary depending on the services utilized. TrueLink will provide Client with an invoice for the requested Hosting Services and Support Services and any other agreed-upon services on a monthly basis. Invoices will be paid within 60 days of receipt.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the minimum monthly fees clause (but not less than $____ per month for the Hosting Services and $____ per month for Support Services), while keeping the reference to exhibits A and B, and then changing the payment terms from 15 to 60 days, a contradiction is introduced. Previously, there was a guaranteed minimum payment due within 15 days. Now, the minimum payment is removed, creating uncertainly, and the payment terms are extended to 60 days. This contradicts the original intention of ensuring a consistent revenue stream for TrueLink, since it no longer exists, but still referring to Exhibits A and B.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Client will be provided upon request with documentation supporting the amount charged (other than the minimum monthly fees listed in Section 4) and will be entitled to contest any charge (other than the minimum monthly fees listed in Section 4), provided that Client timely pays all contested amounts. TrueLink agrees that the costs for any Hosting Services will not increase for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement and that rates charged for Hosting Services will not exceed that charged by TrueLink to any other party.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the minimum monthly fee clause in the changed text creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which explicitly references and relies on those minimum monthly fees. The changed text eliminates the certainty of a minimum payment, while the contradicted text assumes its existence when stating '(other than the minimum monthly fees listed in Section 4)'. This inconsistency makes the 'other than' clause in the contradicted text ambiguous and creates a direct conflict. Furthermore, the extended payment terms exacerbate this issue, as the absence of a minimum payment combined with a longer payment period undermines the original payment expectations, creating more conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Client will use reasonable commercial efforts consistent with the protection of Client Proprietary Information to restrict access to the Interface to its officers, employees, and agents as may be approved by Client.
perturbation.changed_text: Client will use reasonable efforts, but is not obligated, to restrict access to the Interface to its officers, employees, and agents as may be approved by Client.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'but is not obligated' to the clause regarding restricting access to the Interface, the change introduces uncertainty. The original text implied a clear expectation of effort to restrict access. Now, the obligation is weakened, potentially conflicting with the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 10. This is because the standard of "reasonable commercial efforts consistent with the protection of Client Proprietary Information" is being changed with the new clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Client acknowledges its responsibility to preserve the confidentiality of certain technology, information, and documentation embedded in the Interface and agrees to respect the confidential nature of the Interface. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, it is understood and agreed that Client's confidentiality obligations relating to any System and any data, documentation, or other output from such System shall include those specified in any agreements between Client and the licensor of the System.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the client's obligation to restrict access to the Interface, adding 'but is not obligated.' This directly contradicts the original text's implied expectation of effort and introduces ambiguity. This ambiguity clashes with the contradicted text, which states, 'Client acknowledges its responsibility to preserve the confidentiality of certain technology, information, and documentation embedded in the Interface and agrees to respect the confidential nature of the Interface.' The new 'but is not obligated' clause weakens the obligation to protect confidential information, creating a direct contradiction and impacting enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to Section 4 hereof, TrueLink will continue to provide the requested Hosting Services and Support Services until the last day of the month following the month in which Client provides TrueLink with a written notice of its election to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: TrueLink will continue to provide the requested Hosting Services and Support Services until the last day of the month following the month in which Client provides TrueLink with a written notice of its election to terminate this Agreement, but TrueLink can cease providing these services immediately if TrueLink deems Client's use of the Interface to be detrimental to TrueLink's reputation.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the clause 'but TrueLink can cease providing these services immediately if TrueLink deems Client's use of the Interface to be detrimental to TrueLink's reputation,' a new condition for termination is introduced that contradicts the original termination clause, thus creating a in-text contradiction. The addition allows TrueLink to terminate services immediately based on a subjective assessment, conflicting with the original requirement of a written notice period which would potentially expose client to risk, since in one point the client is expecting a notification and in the other there is no notification at all.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will take effect on the Effective Date and remain in effect for a period of 1 year; provided, that Sections 2 and 3 level shall terminate promptly upon (a) any action or omission by Client or any of its customers which constitutes a breach of or default by TrueLink under any System Agreement or Credit Repository Agreement, which breach or default has either not been cured or cannot be cured within the applicable cure period and the consequences of which is that TrueLink will lose material rights it had pursuant to said Agreements or (b) any violations or breach by Client of Sections 8a - 8c. This Agreement shall renew automatically thereafter for successive one year periods until terminated pursuant to Section 12 herein or unless either Client or TrueLink deliver to the other written notice of intent not to renew no later than thirty (30) days prior to the end of said year. If Client decides to discontinue its Hosting Service, Client is responsible for arranging for a new name server within 30 days. In case of discontinued Service, Client shall retain full ownership of all domains associated with Client and Client Content. If Client decides to discontinue Hosting Service, Client is responsible for arranging for a new hosting environment within 60 days.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a new, immediate termination condition based on TrueLink's subjective assessment of Client's Interface use, which directly contradicts the original termination clause requiring written notice and a specified period. Furthermore, it also contradicts the statement in the contradicted text which dictates the conditions under which termination can occur, and the new condition doesn't fit under either scenario laid out in the contradicted text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the client might expect a written notice as stated in the original termination clause, but TrueLink can now terminate immediately without notice, creating an inconsistent and potentially unenforceable agreement. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.
